headlines.title: UN begins evacuation of 11,000 sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.snippet: The UN's maritime agency has initiated an operation to evacuate over 11,000 sailors who are stranded in the Strait of Hormuz. This effort aims to resolve the situation for the large number of mariners affected.

headlines.title: Protesters sentenced to decades in US prison over alleged antifa ties
headlines.snippet: Following a protest outside Prairieland Detention Center, individuals have been sentenced to decades in prison. Civil liberties advocates have voiced concerns regarding this case and its implications.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court says Rastafarian man shaved by prison guards can’t sue
headlines.snippet: The high court has ruled that prisoners cannot sue prison staff for monetary damages under religious liberties law. This decision upholds a previous ruling concerning the rights of incarcerated individuals.

headlines.title: Israel’s deliberate targeting of Palestinian children
headlines.snippet: A new UN report details the impact of Israel's war on Gaza and its occupation of the West Bank on Palestinian children. The report documents the severe toll these actions have taken.

headlines.title: UN: Israel committed genocide by targeting Gaza children
headlines.snippet: According to a UN report, Israel has committed genocide by deliberately targeting children in Gaza. This finding highlights severe human rights concerns in the region.

headlines.title: Israel denying Palestinian women medical care in prison
headlines.snippet: A report from the Palestinian Prisoners and Ex-Prisoners Affairs Commission states that Palestinian women in Israel's Damon Prison are facing dire conditions. They are experiencing severe overcrowding, medical neglect, and denial of basic rights, including for pregnant prisoners.

headlines.title: Ben-Gvir says Israel may ‘act alone’ against Iran in split with US
headlines.snippet: Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir suggested that Israel might have to act independently against Iran's nuclear program. He expressed skepticism about the US expectation that Iran would abandon its nuclear ambitions.

headlines.title: UN report: Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
headlines.snippet: A UN commission of inquiry has concluded that Israel committed genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting Palestinian children. The report indicates that Israeli security forces have intentionally killed and injured children.

headlines.title: Oman announces temporary maritime corridor to bypass Hormuz blockade
headlines.snippet: Oman has announced a temporary maritime corridor to facilitate vessel transit and bypass the Strait of Hormuz blockade. Vessels using this corridor must coordinate with the International Maritime Organisation and Omani authorities.

headlines.title: UN report: 80 percent of Gaza living in dire conditions
headlines.snippet: An assessment from the UN's Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs reveals that approximately 80 percent of Gaza's population is living in dire conditions. Many displaced Palestinians face severe shortages of water, shelter, and basic services.

headlines.title: United States Of America
headlines.snippet: This entry represents a news item about the United States of America.

headlines.title: Without its missiles, Iran would be ‘just like Gaza,’ president says
headlines.snippet: The president stated that Iran, without its missiles, would be comparable to Gaza. This statement draws a parallel between the two regions regarding their military capabilities.

headlines.title: Rubio Says Conflicts Won’t End if Iran’s Proxies Keep Firing
headlines.snippet: Rubio commented that conflicts will persist if Iran's proxies continue their actions. This highlights a concern about ongoing regional instability.

headlines.title: Rubio Says US Will Not Accept Iranian Tolls on Hormuz
headlines.snippet: Rubio stated that the US will not accept Iranian tolls on the Strait of Hormuz. This indicates a clear US stance on maritime passage through the strategic waterway.

headlines.title: Meloni Calls For a Return to Normality After Trump Spat
headlines.snippet: Meloni called for a return to normalcy following a disagreement with Trump. This suggests a diplomatic or political issue that has been resolved or needs resolution.

headlines.title: Rubio rejects Iran tolls on Hormuz as deal strains multiply
headlines.snippet: Rubio rejected the idea of Iran charging tolls on the Strait of Hormuz, stating that no country can levy such fees. Iran's chief negotiator indicated the passage would not revert to its pre-war status.

headlines.title: US Congress passes symbolic Iran war rebuke to Trump
headlines.snippet: The US Congress passed a symbolic resolution to rebuke Trump regarding the war with Iran. This resolution directs Trump to withdraw US forces from hostilities unless Congress explicitly authorizes military action.

headlines.title: Families say Lebanese detainees 'forgotten' in Israel talks
headlines.snippet: Families have stated that Lebanese detainees are being 'forgotten' amidst ongoing discussions. This concern arises as Israeli forces have detained at least 37 individuals from southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: Sudan city 'weeks' from catastrophe: UN official
headlines.snippet: A UN official warned that a city in Sudan is weeks away from catastrophe. The UN highlighted an imminent risk of mass atrocities and urged a withdrawal of forces.

headlines.title: Pro-Israel lobby boasts of role in US sanctions on UN's Albanese
headlines.snippet: Hillel Neuer claimed responsibility for leading a campaign that resulted in US sanctions against UN Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese. This was due to her pro-Palestinian activism.

headlines.snippet: The Foreign Minister and his Moroccan counterpart discussed cooperation and regional developments. This meeting focused on strengthening bilateral ties and addressing current issues.

headlines.snippet: An ambassador stated that Iran will decide how to utilize its unfrozen assets. This indicates Iran's autonomy in managing its financial resources.

headlines.title: Hizbollah chief demands full timetabled Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The chief of Hezbollah is demanding a complete, timetabled withdrawal of Israeli forces from Lebanon. This is a key demand in the ongoing regional tensions.

headlines.snippet: The Crown Prince made a visit to Replit in Silicon Valley. This visit highlights engagement with technology companies.

headlines.snippet: Extremist settlers stormed the Al Aqsa compound, leading to the arrest of 26 Palestinians. This event has escalated tensions in the region.

headlines.snippet: The Lebanese-Syrian Business Council has been launched in Beirut. This initiative aims to foster economic ties between the two countries.

headlines.title: Packaging and packing: Lebanese companies take home awards at major international competition
headlines.snippet: Lebanese companies received awards at a major international competition for their packaging and packing work. This highlights success in the industry.

headlines.title: Israel includes Majdal Zoun in 'buffer zone,' claims Hezbollah tunnel found: Why is it suddenly focusing on the village?
headlines.snippet: The article questions why Israel is suddenly focusing on the village of Majdal Zoun, which it has included in a 'buffer zone.' It also mentions Hezbollah claims of finding a tunnel there.

headlines.title: 'American sponsorship is a guarantee,' says Lebanese-American coordination committee
headlines.snippet: The Lebanese-American coordination committee stated that 'American sponsorship is a guarantee.' This suggests reliance on or expectation of support from the United States.

headlines.snippet: Data indicates that more vessels are transiting the Strait of Hormuz, and Qatar-linked LNG tankers have returned. This suggests a normalization of maritime traffic.

headlines.title: Should Egypt remove ‘marital status’ from women’s ID cards?
headlines.snippet: The article questions whether Egypt should remove 'marital status' from women's ID cards. It discusses how this field can be used to legitimize harassment and restrict opportunities.

headlines.title: The future of oil prices may depend on China
headlines.snippet: The future of oil prices may be influenced by China's demand, even as the US and Iran discuss reopening the Strait of Hormuz. China is noted as the world's second-largest crude oil consumer.

headlines.title: Why the forces that felled Keir Starmer threaten so many Western leaders
headlines.snippet: The article suggests that the factors leading to Keir Starmer's downfall are affecting many Western leaders. It points to a failure to deliver change and a loss of voter trust.

headlines.title: ‘What ceasefire?’: In northern Israel, locals doubt an agreement can end the war with Hezbollah
headlines.snippet: Locals in northern Israel express doubt about whether an agreement can end the war with Hezbollah. The situation in towns near the border remains tense and uncertain.

headlines.title: Israel must have “arms independence,” Netanyahu says, as US military aid expiring in 2028
headlines.snippet: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that Israel needs 'arms independence,' as significant US military aid is set to expire in 2028. He stressed the importance of self-sufficiency in defense.
